entry.title: Add Remote Repository for Pelican `main`
entry.summary:
1. Add the new remote repository:

  2. Go to your local repository in your terminal.

  3. Add the new remote repository using the git remote add command:

bash git remote add new_remote https://github.com/username/new-repo.git

Replace new_remote with the name you want to give to the new remote repository, and …
entry.content:
1. Add the new remote repository:

  2. Go to your local repository in your terminal.

  3. Add the new remote repository using the git remote add command:

bash git remote add new_remote https://github.com/username/new-repo.git

Replace new_remote with the name you want to give to the new remote repository, and https://github.com/username/new-repo.git with the URL of the new remote repository.

  1. Push the main branch to the new remote repository:

  2. Still in your local repository, push the main branch to the new remote repository:

bash git push new_remote main

This will push the main branch to the new remote repository. The gh-pages branch will still be in the old repository.

Now, you have two remote repositories: one for the gh-pages branch (the old repository), and one for the main branch (the new repository). You can version control them independently while maintaining a single local repository.

entry.title: Pelican `make github`
entry.content:
> git clone git@github.com:arulrajnet/attila.git pelican-themes(my directory)
> pelican-themes --install attila --verbose
> make github

Viola!
